docker/Dockerfile.general_idea: Update the build stage base image from Maven with JDK 11 to Maven with Eclipse Temurin JDK 22.
docker/Dockerfile.why_exists: The application code is being migrated to Java 21/22, requiring a compatible JDK for compilation.

docker/Dockerfile.general_idea: Update the deployment stage base image from OpenJDK 14 to Eclipse Temurin JRE 22.
docker/Dockerfile.why_exists: The compiled application requires a Java 21+ runtime environment to execute.

docker/Dockerfile.general_idea: Update the path in the COPY --from=build command to match the new workdir.
docker/Dockerfile.why_exists: The build stage workdir changed from /usr/src/app to /app, so the artifact path must be updated.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project is migrating to JDK 21; without this change, the compiler and build tools will target JDK 17, failing to utilize or enforce JDK 21 features and potentially causing incompatibility with JDK 21-specific dependencies.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Spring Boot version to a release compatible with JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Spring Boot versions may not fully support JDK 21 or may have known incompatibilities; upgrading ensures runtime stability on the new JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Spring Framework version to match the new Spring Boot release.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.4.x requires a compatible Spring Framework version; mismatched versions cause classpath conflicts or runtime errors.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Spring Data version to match the new Spring Boot release.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.4.x requires a compatible Spring Data release train; mismatched versions cause dependency resolution failures or runtime incompatibilities.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace the old Log4j SLF4J bridge with the new SLF4J 2.x compatible bridge.
pom.xml.why_exists: The old `log4j-slf4j-impl` is incompatible with SLF4J 2.x (required by newer Spring Boot/Log4j versions); using it causes `NoSuchMethodError` or classloading failures at runtime.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update SLF4J API version to 2.x to support the new logging bridge.
pom.xml.why_exists: The new `log4j-slf4j2-impl` requires SLF4J 2.x; using the older SLF4J 1.x API causes compilation errors or runtime `NoSuchMethodError`.

src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.general_idea: Update test SQL data to match the new database schema where fees are linked to transactions.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.why_exists: The old INSERT statement targets a table 'fees' and columns (member_id) that no longer exist or have changed in the new schema, causing a runtime SQL error (e.g., Table not found or Column not found) during test execution.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.before: INSERT INTO fees (id, member_id, date, paid) VALUES ...
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.after: INSERT INTO association.transactions ...; INSERT INTO association.fees (id, person_id, date, paid, transaction_id) VALUES ...

src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.general_idea: Update test SQL data to reflect a schema change where fees are now linked to transactions via a foreign key.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.why_exists: The INSERT statement fails with a column count mismatch or missing column error because the 'fees' table schema was altered to include 'transaction_id' and likely renamed to 'association.fees'.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.before: INSERT INTO fees (id, member_id, date, paid) VALUES
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.after: INSERT INTO association.fees (id, person_id, date, paid, transaction_id) VALUES

src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.general_idea: Add test data for the new 'association.transactions' table required by the updated fee model.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.why_exists: The foreign key constraint on 'fees.transaction_id' would fail if the referenced transaction records do not exist in the 'association.transactions' table.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/full_y.after: INSERT INTO association.transactions (id, index, description, amount, date) VALUES

src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/multip.general_idea: Update test SQL data to reflect the new database schema where fees are linked to transactions and persons.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/multip.why_exists: The old INSERT statements target a 'fees' table with a 'member_id' column that no longer exists or has a different structure in the new schema, causing a runtime SQL error (e.g., 'Table not found' or 'Column not found') when tests execute.
src/test/resources/db/queries/fee/multip.after:
INSERT INTO association.transactions (id, index, description, amount, date) VALUES
   (1, 10, 'Transaction', 2, '2020-02-01'),
   (2, 20, 'Transaction', 2, '2020-02-01'),
   (3, 30, 'Transaction', 2, '2020-02-01'),
   (4, 40, 'Transaction', 2, '2020-02-01');

INSERT INTO association.fees (id, person_id, date, paid, transaction_id) VALUES
   (1, 1, '2020-02-01', true, 1),
   (2, 2, '2020-03-01', true, 2),
   (3, 3, '2020-04-01', true, 3),
   (4, 4, '2020-05-01', true, 4),
   (5, 5, '2020-06-01', false, null);
